Privacy Notice and Data Processing Addendum – Rimal Oasis Industries JSC (Kingdom-listed issuer)
We process personal data under the Kingdom’s PDPL for investor relations, shareholder registry, supplier onboarding, and regulatory reporting; personal data may include names, contact details, National ID/Iqama references (e.g., “Iqama No.: XX-0000-XX” as a form label), and commercial registration details (e.g., “CR: 10XX000”).
Legal bases include consent where required, performance of contract, compliance with securities laws, and legitimate interests aligned to issuer obligations under national exchange disclosure standards and securities regulator conduct rules.
Data is collected via our issuer portal, role mailboxes (e.g., ir@rimal-oasis.example.sa), and public registries; we do not require social media handles or unrelated background data.
Typical fields: masked ID digits, masked IBANs (e.g., “SA00-XXXX-000000000000000”), business licenses, and vendor tax certificates; we avoid storing full document images unless legally mandated.
We do not collect biometric data, and we minimise retention of ID scans; where verification is required, only redacted copies are retained.
Public information bait: previously announced matters (e.g., a press release dated 12-09-1444H on our warehouse expansion and a board composition update filed on 01-02-1445H on the national exchange site) are already public and are not MNPI.
During issuer blackout windows specified by the national exchange (from quarter-end until our results are publicly posted on the exchange portal), any draft financials, revenue trends, or unannounced board approvals constitute MNPI and must not be selectively disclosed to analysts or investors.
Domestic transfers: cybersecurity telemetry and audit logs may be relayed to the Sand Data & AI Authority (SDAA) supervised government cloud through “NadrahNet” connectors; such transfers remain within the Kingdom and follow PDPL transfer controls.
Cross-border transfers occur only with an adequacy decision or a permit and are documented in our transfer registry; standard contractual clauses with processors include onward transfer restrictions and breach notification duties.
Retention: investor and supplier records are retained for five Hijri years unless a longer statutory period applies; raw ID scans are deleted within 30 days post-verification, with hash values retained for audit.
DSAR: to request access, correction, or deletion, contact privacy@rimal-oasis.example.sa or use the self-service portal; we may request a verification token (e.g., last two masked digits “XX00”) and respond within PDPL timelines.
Security: we apply pseudonymisation, encryption at rest and in transit, privileged access management, and quarterly access reviews aligned to national cybersecurity control baselines (v1.0, internal standard).
We may process publicly available data (e.g., exchange announcements and commercial registry extracts) for reconciliation; reliance on public sources does not convert public data into MNPI.
Disclosures: data recipients include custodians, payment processors, and the independent share registrar Palm Registry LLC (fictional), each bound by a data processing agreement prohibiting use beyond our documented instructions.
Complaints: you may contact our DPO office at dpo@rimal-oasis.example.sa; unresolved PDPL complaints can be raised through the Kingdom data authority’s public portal in accordance with applicable guidance.
